title: Char Bangla Temples
tagline: Char Bangla Temples — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Char Bangla Temples, are located at Baranagar in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Bhavaniswar Mandir
topPlaces.description: Bhavaniswar Mandir, is located at Baranagar in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Baranagar, Murshidabad
topPlaces.description: Baranagar (also referred to as Baronagar, Barnagar) is a village in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the state of West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Murshidabad-Jiaganj
topPlaces.description: Murshidabad-Jiaganj is a community development block that forms an administrative division in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Jiaganj Azimganj
topPlaces.description: Jiaganj Azimganj is a city and a municipality in Murshidabad district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Nashipur
topPlaces.description: Nashipur is a village in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block in the Lalbag subdivision of Murshidabad district in West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Kathgola Palace
topPlaces.description: Kathgola Palace is a historical building belonging to the Dugar family at Kathgola in the Murshidabad-Jiaganj CD block of Murshidabad district. It now houses a museum.

topPlaces.name: Kathgola
topPlaces.description: Kathgola (also known as Katgola) is a neighbourhood in the city of Murshidabad which was at one time the capital of Bengal, Bihar and Orissa during the reign of the Nawabs of Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Namak Haram Deorhi
topPlaces.description: Namak Haram Deorhi (Bengali: নিমোক হারাম দেওরহি, lit. 'Traitor's Gate'), originally known as Jafragunj Deohri, was the palace of Mir Jafar. It is located in Murshidabad, just opposite to the Jafarganj Cemetery in the Lalbagh area, in the Indian state of West Bengal.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Char Bangla Temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Char Bangla Temples is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Char Bangla Temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (179 km) and the nearest railway station is Katihar Junction (158 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Char Bangla Temples?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Char Bangla Temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Char Bangla Temples?
faq.a: Stays in Char Bangla Temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Char Bangla Temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Bhavaniswar Mandir, Baranagar, Murshidabad, Murshidabad-Jiaganj, Jiaganj Azimganj, Nashipur.
